meta.title: Pattern Studio — document mining
meta.note: No live LLM in this build. The checklist, clarifiers, maturity scores, KPI gaps and candidate selection are all derived from the corpus, deterministically. fewShotPrompt is the exact prompt a real extractor would receive — wire it in at PIQ.patternStudio.mine().

steps.label: Context
steps.blurb: What function, and what are you trying to achieve?

steps.label: Documents
steps.blurb: The evidence we need, and what you have.

steps.label: Results
steps.blurb: Maturity, benchmarks, and the patterns that close the gap.

stages.label: Ingest & Classify
stages.blurb: Parse each document; pull tables, reason codes, amounts, dates and freetext.

stages.label: Signal extraction
stages.blurb: Find what recurs across the corpus: reason codes, timings, triggers, entities.

stages.label: Pattern hypothesis
stages.blurb: Few-shot against the existing library — propose patterns it does not yet hold.

stages.label: SME review
stages.blurb: You are the gate. Accept, edit, merge or reject each candidate.

stages.label: Calibration
stages.blurb: Run the accepted detector against the uploaded transactions to set a threshold.

dimensions.label: Process Standardization
dimensions.probe: are processes documented, standardized, and actually followed?

dimensions.label: Data & Analytics Maturity
dimensions.probe: are KPIs tracked, automated, predictive — or hand-compiled?

dimensions.label: Technology Enablement
dimensions.probe: ERP, automation, workflow and integration maturity.

dimensions.label: Governance & Controls
dimensions.probe: SOX, segregation of duties, control monitoring, audit readiness.

process.present: process documentation found — steps and owners are traceable
process.absent: no process documentation uploaded — steps and owners are unverifiable

data.present: KPI/analytics exports present — metrics exist, though compiled manually
data.absent: no KPI or analytics exports — the function is flying on gut feel

tech.present: system exports present — the ERP is emitting usable event data
tech.absent: no system/ERP exports — automation potential cannot be assessed

people.present: org/RACI evidence present — accountability is at least declared
people.absent: no org or RACI evidence — accountability gaps are invisible

governance.present: control/audit evidence present — the control set is documented
governance.absent: no control or audit evidence — SOX and SoD posture is unknown

slots.label: Exception logs
slots.hint: Blocked invoices, failed postings, held orders, credit check failures

slots.label: Aging reports with collector notes
slots.hint: The freetext fields — where the real story lives

slots.label: Process mining exports

slots.label: Cycle time / touch-count

slots.label: RACI matrix

slots.label: Invoice exception / block logs

slots.label: Off-contract / maverick spend
slots.hint: Spend that bypassed the negotiated rate

slots.label: Delegation of authority matrix
slots.hint: Approval thresholds by role and value

slots.label: AP KPI reports
slots.hint: DPO, touchless rate, cost per invoice

slots.label: Close task checklist

slots.label: Close KPI reports
slots.hint: Days to close, entries per FTE, error rate

slots.label: Flux / variance analysis

slots.label: Stock level snapshots
slots.hint: Weekly/monthly exports by SKU and site

slots.label: Demand forecast vs actuals
slots.hint: Where the plan and the world diverged

slots.label: Safety stock parameter tables

slots.label: Purchase order history with lead times
Carrying cost / obsolescence reports

o2c.q: Which ERP is this running on? It changes the report names we ask for.
What is the primary driver of the delay you are seeing?
options: Not sure — that's what I want to find out

clusters.name: Intercompany Reconciliation Delays
clusters.patterns: Matching failures, balance discrepancies, late confirmations

clusters.name: Accrual Accuracy & Reversal
clusters.patterns: Stale accruals, reversal timing drift, manual top-ups

clusters.name: Subledger Reconciliation Bottlenecks
docs: Close task checklist with actual completion times

blueprints.title: DSO reduction
blueprints.focus: Collections behaviour + dispute cycle + cash application

clusters.name: Customer Payment Behaviour
clusters.patterns: Stalling tactics, broken promises, term creep

clusters.name: Cash Application Drag
clusters.patterns: Remittance format churn, unapplied cash ageing

clusters.name: Replenishment & Supplier Reliability
docs: Purchase order history with lead times

candidates.name: Seasonal Treasury Squeeze
candidates.mentalModel: They pay us fine for ten months, then quarter-end lands and suddenly everyone is 'awaiting approval'. It is not distress — it is their treasury calendar, and we are the float.

branchingFlow.condition: IF Skew > 9 days AND Payment_History_Otherwise_Clean
IF Skew > 20 days AND Exposure > $500K
IF Skew rising QoQ for 3 quarters

evidence.excerpt: "Customer AP confirms invoice approved — payment run moved to next quarter per their treasury policy." (recurs 34× across Q1–Q3 notes)
142 held/late events cluster in the final 10 days of Mar / Jun / Sep — 3.1× the base rate.
Days-to-pay rises from 41 to 58 in quarter-end weeks, then reverts.

candidates.name: Remittance Format Churn
candidates.mentalModel: Every month the remittance file looks different. Not corrupt — different. Enough to break auto-match and buy them a week of float while we key it by hand.

branchingFlow.condition: IF Schema_Variants >= 3 AND Auto_Match_Rate < 40%
IF Churn persists 3 months AND Unapplied > $250K

evidence.excerpt: 4 payers account for 61% of auto-match failures; each shows ≥3 distinct remittance layouts in 6 months.
214 unapplied cash lines, 88% traced to remittance parse failure rather than payment shortfall.

candidates.name: Approval Threshold Shadowing
candidates.mentalModel: Nobody breaks the approval rule. They just never quite reach it. Every credit memo lands a few hundred dollars under the line that would have sent it to me.

branchingFlow.condition: IF Amount within 10% below threshold AND Approver_Repeat_Count > 5
IF Cluster spans multiple approvers on one customer

evidence.excerpt: "Credit memos above $25,000 require Controller counter-signature." (Write-off policy v4.2, §3.1)
Internal audit FY24-07: "a material share of memos fall in the $22.5K–$25K band; management response pending."
2 credit analysts hold both memo-raise and memo-approve rights.

candidates.name: Escalation Dead-Zone
candidates.mentalModel: The playbook says escalate at day 45. The collectors escalate at day 70. That 25-day gap is where the money goes quiet — and nobody owns it.

branchingFlow.condition: IF Lag > 14 days AND Balance > $50K
IF Lag > 30 days AND Customer_On_Watchlist

evidence.excerpt: "Accounts 45 days past due are escalated to the Collections Manager." (Escalation protocol, §2)
Median first manager-level contact occurs at day 70; 210 accounts sat 45–70 days with no logged action.

candidates.name: Demand Forecast Override Bias
candidates.mentalModel: The statistical forecast was fine. Then a planner nudged it up 'to be safe', every month, in the same direction. The bias is not in the model — it is in the override.

branchingFlow.condition: IF Override_Delta positive in >70% of periods
IF Bias persists 2 quarters AND MAPE worsens

evidence.excerpt: Final forecast exceeds statistical baseline in 11 of 14 months; mean override +8.4%, and actuals land below both.
"Agreed to hold the higher number to protect service level." (recurs in 6 of 9 S&OP minutes)
Safety stock parameters revised upward twice, never downward.

candidates.name: Slow-Moving Inventory Creep
candidates.mentalModel: Nothing is ever declared obsolete. It just gets quietly older, one reclassification at a time, until it is written off in a year nobody is accountable for.

branchingFlow.condition: IF Zero movement 90+ days AND Value > $25K
IF Zero movement 180+ days OR Cohort > $500K

evidence.excerpt: 22% of SKUs show no movement in 90+ days, carrying $4.1M at standard cost.
MB51 shows 340 SKUs with zero goods issues in 6 months, yet still replenished by MRP.

candidates.name: Supplier Lead-Time Drift
candidates.mentalModel: The master data says the lead time is 14 days. It has actually been 23 for a year. We are not carrying safety stock for demand volatility — we are carrying it for a supplier nobody re-baselined.

branchingFlow.condition: IF Actual_LT exceeds Master_LT by >30% for 3 months
IF OTIF < 85% AND Spend > $1M

candidates.estImpact: -12 days safety stock · $1.8M working capital release

evidence.excerpt: Mean actual lead time 23.4 days against a master-data value of 14; drift stable for 12 months.
OTIF 84% overall; the 6 worst suppliers account for 71% of the misses.

candidates.name: Intercompany Confirmation Lag
candidates.mentalModel: Both sides booked it. Neither side confirmed it. The close waits five days for two people to agree on a number they both already know.

branchingFlow.condition: IF Variance < tolerance AND Both_Sides_Booked
IF Variance > tolerance OR Aged > 2 cycles

evidence.excerpt: 3 mismatches open at D+2 across the EMEA/APAC leg; 61% of IC pairs sit within a 0.5% tolerance band yet remain unconfirmed.
IC elimination is on the critical path in all 3 sampled cycles, slipping D+2 → D+3.

candidates.name: Stale Accrual Carry-Forward
candidates.mentalModel: The accrual was raised for a reason that expired two quarters ago. It rolls forward every month because reversing it is somebody's job and nobody's priority.

branchingFlow.condition: IF Accrual age > 2 periods AND Trigger_Event_Closed
IF Value > materiality OR Basis_Unclear

evidence.excerpt: 46% of journal entries are manual; 118 recur monthly with an identical description and value.
Accrual accounts carry 31 items aged beyond 2 periods, none with a documented reversal trigger.

candidates.name: Threshold-Split Requisitioning
candidates.mentalModel: Two requisitions, same vendor, same day, each just under the approval line. It is not fraud — it is a buyer routing around a control they find inconvenient.

branchingFlow.condition: IF 2+ reqs same vendor within 72h AND Sum > threshold
IF Requester repeats across 3 periods

evidence.excerpt: 22% of spend is off-contract; 148 requisition pairs sit within 72h of each other for the same vendor, each below the $10K line.
"Requisitions above $10,000 require Director approval." (DoA matrix v3, row 12)

candidates.name: GR/IR Ageing Blind Spot
candidates.mentalModel: Goods received, invoice never matched — or invoice matched, goods never received. Either way it sits in GR/IR until someone writes it off in a quarter nobody is watching.

branchingFlow.condition: IF Receipt and Invoice both present AND Variance < tolerance
IF Aged > 90 days

evidence.excerpt: 18% invoice exception rate; GR/IR carries 412 open items, 37% aged beyond 90 days.
Receipt posted without invoice in 210 cases; the reverse in 96.

fewShotPrompt:
You are a process transformation expert analysing discovery documents to identify
behavioural patterns in {function} processes.

Client context
  Function:   {function}
  Objective:  {objective}
  Clarified:  {clarified}

Here are examples of known patterns from the existing library:
{examples}

Now analyse the following documents and signals:
{signals}

Identify NEW behavioural patterns not already captured in the library above.
For each candidate pattern, provide:
  1. name            — a concise descriptive name
  2. featureSlug     — kebab-case identifier
  3. description     — what this pattern represents and why it matters
  4. triggers        — observable conditions that indicate this pattern
  5. processFlow     — the happy-path action-block sequence (straight-through resolution)
  6. branchingFlow   — variation branches with tiers (primary/escalation/immediate),
                       action blocks, executor types (Agent/Workflow/Script/Human Task),
                       and HITL gates
  7. evidence        — which documents and signals support this pattern
  8. confidence      — 0-1 score for how strongly the evidence supports this
  9. similarTo       — if this resembles an existing pattern, which one and how
 10. servingMissions — which Command Centre missions (M1–M60) this pattern would serve
 11. servingThemes   — which themes (A–H) this pattern maps to

Structure each candidate pattern EXACTLY like the examples above.
